ground_truth.chief_complaint: Annual diabetes review with poor glycaemic control

symptoms.name: nocturia
symptoms.detail: up 2-3 times per night to urinate

symptoms.name: foot numbness
symptoms.detail: intermittent; doctor notes it could be an early nerve effect of diabetes (turn 5)

relevant_history: Poor adherence - sometimes misses the evening metformin (stated turn 2)
Eating out a lot due to work stress (stated turn 2)
Not self-monitoring blood glucose - ran out of test strips (stated turn 6)

recent_results.source: stated turn 3; values also in patient context

diagnosis.primary: Type 2 diabetes - inadequately controlled (HbA1c 72 vs target ~53, stated turn 3)
secondary: Possible early diabetic nerve effect in the feet (doctor's stated concern, turn 5)

ordered_tests: Foot examination with sensation check (stated turn 5)

prescribed_medication.indication: lowers sugar, protects heart and kidneys (doctor's explanation, turn 7)

follow_up: Re-supply blood glucose test strips and resume home monitoring ('let's fix that', turn 7)
Patient agreed to start the new medications (turn 10)
patient_education: Sick-day advice for empagliflozin - tell the team / it may be paused if unwell, unable to eat or drink, or very high sugars (turn 9)
Empagliflozin causes more urination and a little extra sugar; keep the genital area clean to avoid thrush (turn 9)

clinical_note.subjective: 61M with 8-year T2DM (per context) for annual review. Admits missing the evening metformin, eating out, and weight gain. Reports increased thirst, nocturia 2-3x/night, and intermittent toe numbness. Not self-monitoring (out of strips).
clinical_note.objective: BP 142/88. HbA1c 72 mmol/mol (8.7%), target ~53. Foot exam to be performed this visit.
clinical_note.assessment: Inadequately controlled T2DM with hyperglycaemic symptoms and possible early foot nerve involvement; raised blood pressure.
clinical_note.plan: Continue metformin; start empagliflozin and a low dose of ramipril. Foot exam today. Refer to diabetes nurse and dietitian. Re-supply test strips. Sick-day and hygiene advice given; leaflet provided.
